title: What did Ong mean by "aggregat...
first_prompt: What did Ong mean by "aggregative thought"?
summary: Irwin was trying to understand Walter Ong's concept of "aggregative thought," specifically what the term meant in the context of oral cultures building knowledge. The conversation clarified that aggregation involves clustering experience itself, rather than separating ideas analytically, and provided examples of how qualities fuse with nouns, such as "wine-dark sea." The discussion concluded by applying this framework to modern English, identifying hip hop and rap as a rich living tradition where rappers fuse identity and quality into name-epithets, citing examples like "Notorious B.I.G." and "Ol' Dirty Bastard."
